_comment: STRESS-CASE rig for examples/drawings/snowmen-scene.jpg (1200x900). This is a NEGATIVE fixture: it documents a drawing the Animator should REFUSE, and it exists so the bail path can be tested. It is a good-faith attempt to rig the red/green snowman as a biped and it does NOT work, for two stacked reasons recorded here. (1) key() does not separate this image at all: the paper is a warm-lit photograph (blank paper sat~36-70, well over the colorness threshold of 20), so `colorness` fires on the PAPER and the raw mask keeps 93.5% of the page. closing+fill_holes then merges the whole sheet into ONE blob, so largest-blob isolation is a no-op and every polygon below clips cream paper plus whatever else it overlaps. (2) Even after per-channel white-balance against the paper illuminant (which drops the mask to 37.7% and yields 32 blobs), the largest blob is the BLUE WATER BAND (49.4% of ink, aspect 2.98, x[15:1188]) with both snowmen fused to it, because they touch the water. The snowman is never independently obtainable via largest-blob. (3) A snowman has no legs -- it is a stack of balls -- so there is no leg-like protrusion to gait and no walk cycle to author; only the arms/hat below can move. Do not ship this rig as a success. See the bail signals in the report.
name: Snow Man (red)
vibe: cannot walk, has no legs, is standing in the sea
